title: Daily Git workflow with topic branches
context: Used Git for personal, academic, and work repositories with consistent topic-branch hygiene.
details: Default workflow: short-lived topic branch, frequent commits with descriptive messages, PR with self-review, squash merge. Rebased stale branches before re-review.
outcome: Consistent Git hygiene across personal, coursework, and work repos.

title: Linux and WSL as primary dev environment
context: Set up Ubuntu on WSL as the primary development environment for coursework and personal projects.
details: Configured zsh, tmux, and a shared dotfiles repo. Used systemd services for the Jupyter and Streamlit dev servers.
outcome: Stable Linux-based development environment across coursework and side projects.

title: Containerized coursework projects
context: Wrote Dockerfiles for two of the coursework projects to make grading reproducible.
details: Used multi-stage builds to keep the final image under 300 MB. Pinned base image digests for reproducibility.
outcome: Two projects shipped as reproducible Docker images.

title: Set up GitHub Actions for personal projects
context: Configured GitHub Actions workflows for two personal projects.
details: Ran the pytest suite on every push. Added a separate workflow that built and pushed Docker images on tagged releases.
outcome: Two personal projects running continuous integration.

title: LaTeX for academic reports
context: Wrote technical reports and seminar deliverables in LaTeX.
details: Used a shared lab template, BibTeX for citations, and tikz for a handful of figures. Compiled locally with latexmk.
outcome: All seminar reports and one publication-style report delivered in LaTeX.

title: English and German technical communication
context: Wrote technical reports in English and status updates in German in workplace contexts.
details: Used English for code comments, PR descriptions, and academic writing. Used German for standups, retrospectives, and stakeholder updates in the working-student role.
outcome: Effective contributor in English-language academic and German-language workplace environments.
